paragraph: Single-family homes in Garfield are moving with real momentum. The median sale price is $557,500, and 77 percent of homes are selling above asking with a sale-to-list ratio of 1.03. Median days on market sits at 21, which means priced homes are moving without excessive wait. This is a competitive but balanced market where sellers have some advantage, yet buyers with well-positioned offers can still compete. Multi-family properties, by contrast, are performing even stronger. With 27 sales over six months at a median of $757,000, they're drawing consistent interest. The sale-to-list ratio climbs to 1.04 and 70 percent of these properties sell above asking, all within a median of 22 days on market. Condos and townhouses tell a different story. Only 10 sales in the same period at a median of $525,000, with a sale-to-list ratio of 0.99 and 30 percent selling above asking. Median days on market stretch to 37 and a half, signaling that buyers have real leverage here and sellers need to price competitively to move inventory. If you're weighing which property type makes sense, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family or multi-family investment, or the lower entry point of a condo or townhouse.
